_comment_ApiKeys: Do NOT store real API keys here. Provide them via user-secrets or environment variables, e.g. ApiKeys__<key>=<projectName>. GitHub__WebhookSecret and SaaS__TenantRoot are configured the same way.

SemanticAnalyzer._comment: NumCtx: the model context window (tokens) requested from Ollama for RAG answers. The prompt is budgeted to leave room for the answer; raising it lets more context nodes through (default 8192). Set it to match the served model's window.

Rag._comment: MaxContextNodes: hard cap on node ids per /api/ask before the token budget prunes further (default 20). MinRelevanceScore: absolute relevance floor for context nodes when the client sends scores; below it a node is dropped, and if none clear it the server abstains without an LLM call. 0 = off (default) until calibrated for your retriever's score scale.

Embedding._comment: Source of the vector text for semantic search: 'code' (default; type+name+signature+summary+bounded body) retrieves far better on natural-language queries than 'summary' (the 1-sentence AI summary alone).

Features._comment: StreamingAnswers: stream the Ask-AI answer token-by-token (default true). Set false to return the full answer in one response.

SemanticEnrichment._comment: Background enrichment worker. BatchSize = nodes pulled per poll cycle; MaxParallelism = concurrent nodes per batch (client-side concurrency is safe — Ollama serializes internally when it can't parallelize). Lower MaxParallelism to 1 on memory-constrained backends.

Indexing._comment: SemanticCSharp: GLOBAL default for exact C# resolution (REFERENCES_TYPE/IMPLEMENTS/EXTENDS/CALLS via a Roslyn SemanticModel; ambiguous name-matching skipped). More accurate but heavier (compilation per scan); ON by default — it is what earns EXTRACTED provenance. Set false here (or "SemanticCSharp": false per project in projects.json) to opt out on very large repos. The CLI honours SHONKOR_SEMANTIC_CSHARP=false to opt out.

Drift._comment: Drift Layer 3: background reconciliation. ReconcileIntervalSeconds > 0 enables a worker that periodically reconciles each project's graph against its working tree (re-indexing only drifted files), catching out-of-band edits (git pull, branch switch, external editor). 0 = disabled (default); it hashes candidate files each cycle, so pick an interval suited to repo size.
